intro: Hemipilia alpestris, also known as Himalayan Hemipilia, is a species of orchid that grows in the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemipilia alpestris include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hemipilia alpestris is often made into decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and sore throats.
